examples.text:
وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ خَاشِعَةٌ
 عَامِلَةٌ نَاصِبَةٌ
 تَصْلَى نَارًا حَامِيَةً
 تُسْقَى مِنْ عَيْنٍ آنِيَةٍ
 لَيْسَ لَهُمْ طَعَامٌ إِلَّا مِنْ ضَرِيعٍ
 لَا يُسْمِنُ وَلَا يُغْنِي مِنْ جُوعٍ
examples.english:
[Some] faces, that Day, will be humbled,
Working [hard] and exhausted.
They will [enter to] burn in an intensely hot Fire.
They will be given drink from a boiling spring.
For them there will be no food except from a poisonous, thorny plant
Which neither nourishes nor avails against hunger.

senses.gloss: vehement, ardent, fiery, heated, violent, forceful (of an emotion such as anger)

examples.text:
حُمَاةَ الْحِمَى يَا حُمَاةَ الْحِمَى
 هَلُمُّوا هَلُمُّوا لِمَجْدِ الزَّمَنْ
examples.english:
O defenders of the Homeland
 rally around to the glory of our time!

examples.text: مَا جَعَلَ اللّٰهُ مِن بَحِيرَةٍ وَلَا سَائِبَةٍ وَلَا وَصِيلَةٍ وَلَا حَامٍ

senses.gloss: Ḥām, the son of Nūḥ and the brother of Yāfiṯ and Sām.

etymology.text:
Compare حَمِيم (ḥamīm).

From the active participle of حَمَى (ḥamā, “to protect”).
The sense ‘stallion-camel freed from work’ has been explained as coming from the image of a stallion-camel protecting its back: "a stallion-camel that has prohibited, or interdicted, his back [to be used for bearing a rider or any burden]".

From Biblical Hebrew חָם (ḥām).
